title: `host.cgroupControllers` is missing cpu on rootless
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

This seems to be similar problem to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11931 in that when invoking `podman info` I'm not getting all the information:
```
$ podman info --format json
{
  "host": {
    "arch": "amd64",
    "buildahVersion": "1.26.1",
    "cgroupManager": "systemd",
    "cgroupVersion": "v2",
    "cgroupControllers": [
      "memory",
      "pids"
    ],
```

but

```
$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/cgroup.controllers
cpuset cpu io memory pids
```
and
```
$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/cgroup.subtree_control
cpuset cpu io memory pids
```

both have cpu. Unfortunately this prevents [kind](https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/) from working properly,
because it checks for memory, pids and cpu being present there. 

**Describe the results you expected:**

`podman info` to contain:
```
"cgroupControllers": [
      "memory",
      "pids",
      "cpu",
    ],
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.18.3
Built:        Wed Jun 15 16:31:58 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

No

physical

comments.body:
I spoke with @giuseppe and he helped me figured it out. In my case the problem was that my service cgroup `cat /proc/self/cgroup` did not have the `cpu` in it. The two possible approaches that worked for me are:
1. add `Delegate=true` in that particular service as described in https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/docs/user/rootless/
2. run a new shell in a new systemd scope `systemd-run --scope --user -p Delegate=true bash`
